# GEO/SEO Research Brief — 2026-05-25

**Reference UTC:** 2026-05-25 12:00 UTC
**Generated by:** Penny SEO/GEO Research Agent

---

## Top 3 Findings

### 1. Google I/O 2026: AI Search Overhaul is LIVE (May 19)

Google's "intelligent search box" — the biggest change to Search in 25+ years — is rolling out globally. Key changes:
- **Gemini 3.5 Flash** is the new default model in AI Mode (1B+ MAUs, queries doubling quarterly)
- The search box now **expands for conversational queries**, supports multi-modal input (text, images, files, video, Chrome tabs)
- **Information agents** arriving this summer — users can create agents that run 24/7 tracking changes and alerting
- Follow-up questions now possible directly from AI Overviews
- The era of "ten blue links" is **officially over** per TechCrunch

> **Signal:** If you're not optimizing for AI citation and conversational discovery, traffic will continue declining.

### 2. llms.txt: Crawlers Aren't Reading It — But Ship It Anyway

An empirical study of 500M+ LLM bot traffic events (Limy, May 2026) found that GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, OAI-SearchBot, and Google-Extended are **almost never fetching /llms.txt** — they're crawling HTML directly. Adoption rate: ~10% of domains.

**However:** llms.txt is not an SEO play — it's a **Business-to-Agent (B2A)** play. It's the first standardized way for brands to publish a machine-readable surface for AI agents to route on. The companies treating it as agentic web infrastructure are measuring the right outcome.

> **Signal:** Don't expect llms.txt to improve AI search citations (yet). Do expect it to matter for agentic discovery as AI systems mature.

### 3. robots.txt is Broken for AI — ai.txt Emerging as Standard

A Duke University study (2025) found many AI crawlers **don't even request robots.txt**. The limitations:
- Can only control access, not usage (can't say "index for search, not training")
- No semantic layer — treats entire URLs equally
- Relies on voluntary compliance which AI crawlers ignore

New alternatives gaining traction:
- **ai.txt** — Domain-specific language for AI interactions (text allowed, images forbidden, etc.)
- **agentic-robots.txt** — robots.txt + MCP manifest for AI agents
- Natural-language instructions for compliant AI agents

> **Signal:** Your robots.txt alone is insufficient. Consider ai.txt as a supplement for AI-specific policy.

## Action Items for PSEO Network Template

1. **Add multi-modal content optimization** — Images, files, video as search inputs is now a Google feature; ensure content is structured for non-text queries
2. **Prioritize structured data for AI citation** — With AI Mode and agentic search, schema markup that AI systems can verify and cite is critical (Google dropped 7 schema types, but citation-focused markup remains key)
3. **llms.txt is infrastructure, not SEO** — Update template guidance: implement for B2A agentic routing, not AI search ranking
4. **Prepare for information agents** — Content needs to be machine-digestible for agents that run 24/7 monitoring use cases
